name: Nicholas Hall

points: Spearhead company-wide accessibility practice, establishing standards, automated testing, and developer training
Consult directly with clients and their internal accessibility teams to assess compliance, define remediation strategies, and support VPAT documentation
Remediate front-end accessibility issues across multiple codebases, scanning tools, and frameworks, ranging from surface-level content fixes to complex structural overhauls
Translate complex or unconventional designs into fully accessible implementations without compromising creativity or user experience
Continuously build and share accessibility knowledge through self-directed research, industry conferences, and webinars, ensuring the team stays current on WCAG and ADA developments
Built websites for clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies to startups, across higher education, energy, and arts sectors
Contributed to award-winning, fully accessible work for Ivy League institutions, top liberal arts colleges, and Fortune 500 clients, recognized by the Webby Awards, UCDA, CASE, and Davey Awards
Created an internal codebase focused on standardizing solutions and reducing the time needed to start new projects
Interviewed, onboarded, trained, and mentored new developers
Developed, documented, and maintained code standards, making it easier to assist, review, and debug each other's work
Worked with the product team to identify potential issues with scope, accessibility, and project timeline
Teamed up with design and strategy to discover creative solutions to complex problems while achieving the desired user experience and sense of emotion
Led department-wide process and workflow improvements that increased efficiency without sacrificing quality
Implemented a new project management system to improve communication and accountability across teams
Managed a team of local and remote developers, allocating resources based on individual strengths and growth areas
Drove hiring decisions for interns and full-time developers, including interviews and onboarding
Conducted performance reviews and communicated career accomplishments to senior management
Managed all aspects of site production from design concepts through post-launch maintenance
Built and maintained websites for clients in higher education, energy, and arts sectors
Championed progressive enhancement techniques that improved development efficiency and user experience
Served as liaison between front-end and back-end teams to ensure cohesive, high-quality output
Participated in remote projects for accelerated project schedules (ask me about Bucharest or Honolulu)
Served as primary front-end developer for complex CSS layouts and web application prototypes
Built and maintained sites using both proprietary and open source CMS platforms
Ensured 508 and ADA compliance on all new builds

education.name: Towson University

points: Graduated with magna cum laude honors

certifications: Certified Professional in Accessibility Core Competencies (CPACC)
skills: I maintain excellent written and oral communication skills as well as understanding and listening skills. I am passionate about my work, eager to learn, and pay attention to details. I am a problem solver with a substantial disregard for the impossible.
